metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe AC2 as a concrete structural check, e.g. 'README.md retains headings (or equivalent sections) covering: what rebar is, installation, quickstart, and routing links' — evaluable by a grep/heading scan.
findings.finding: AC2 uses the evaluative phrase 'stands alone as an intro' without a deterministic, binary check. 'Stands alone' is a human judgment that different reviewers will apply differently.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace 'The README still stands alone as an intro (what/install/quickstart/routing)' with: 'README.md retains dedicated sections for: project description, installation, quickstart, and routing links (verifiable by section-heading grep).'

Proving command: `grep -E '^#+ *(Install|Quick[Ss]tart|Rout)' README.md`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether a docs link/index CI gate actually exists (no such gate was found in any workflow); if it does not, either add one (e.g. lychee or markdown-link-check as a CI step) or rewrite AC 3 to name the concrete manual or automated check that will verify no dead links are introduced.
findings.finding: AC line 3 asserts 'No dead links (passes the docs link/index CI gate)' but no such docs link/index CI gate exists in the repository. A search across all CI workflow files (.github/workflows/test.yml, gerrit-verify.yaml, and all other workflows), the Makefile, and .pre-commit-config.yaml found no link-checker step (no lychee, no markdown-link-check, no link-index drift gate, no dead-link validation). Asserting that this AC is satisfied by passing a non-existent gate leaves it unverifiable and potentially silently unmet.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'No dead links (passes the docs link/index CI gate)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'passes the docs link/index CI gate' with a concrete, existing check — e.g. 'All URLs in README.md that point to generated reference pages resolve to real file paths on the `docs/` branch (verified by a new `make check-links` step or lychee CI step added as part of this work)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) remove the 'docs link/index CI gate' reference from AC3 and replace it with a concrete manual verification step, or (b) note that this gate is being created by a dependency ticket and name that ticket.
findings.finding: AC3 asserts 'passes the docs link/index CI gate' as a verification mechanism, but no such gate exists in the repo's CI workflows (test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml were fully searched — neither contains a link-checking step).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either point to an existing CI link-checker gate by name/job, or add a concrete verification step (e.g. a script run in CI or a manual command) that actually checks markdown link targets in README.md — AC3 must name an enforceable mechanism, not assume a non-existent gate.
findings.finding: AC3 asserts 'No dead links (passes the docs link/index CI gate)' as the verification mechanism, but no such gate exists in the codebase. Examining test.yml and all scripts shows the CI suite contains drift gates for env-vars, types, prompt-index, criteria-routing, and server manifest — none of which check markdown hyperlinks in README.md. The plan's no-dead-links guarantee relies on a gate that is either absent or undefined, leaving AC3 unenforceable as written.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'passes the docs link/index CI gate' in AC3 with either: (a) the name of a specific existing CI check that covers README.md markdown links, or (b) a concrete manual or scripted verification step (e.g. 'run `python scripts/check_readme_links.py` — to be added — and confirm exit 0'), and add that script/step to the Files section if it needs to be created.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the 'docs link/index CI gate' exists (or identify which ticket creates it) before AC3 can be treated as a valid acceptance test; if it does not yet exist, either add link-checking to this plan's scope or restate AC3 as a manual verification step.
findings.finding: AC3 ('No dead links — passes the docs link/index CI gate') asserts a CI gate exists to verify link validity, but no such docs link-checker gate is present in any .github/workflows/ file. The plan stakes its dead-link acceptance criterion on an unverified infrastructure capability.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'No dead links (passes the docs link/index CI gate)'
findings.suggested_fix: Either reference the specific ticket/step that creates the docs link-checker gate, or add a concrete fallback: 'manually verify all introduced links resolve to existing files before merging, pending a future link-check CI gate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC1 (e.g. `grep -c 'docs/cli-reference' README.md` to confirm links were inserted) and AC2 (e.g. `grep -qE '^## (Install|Quick[Ss]tart|Routing)' README.md` to confirm required sections are present).
findings.finding: AC1 ('volatile catalogs are removed and replaced with links') and AC2 ('README still stands alone as an intro') have no stated proving command. Only AC3 has a concrete CI gate. Without a proving command for AC1, completion of the removal is asserted, not verified; AC2 has no deterministic check at all.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before removing README sections, audit other docs/CLAUDE.md/contributing guides for inbound anchor links to the catalog sections being removed, and update or redirect those references.
findings.finding: The plan removes volatile catalog sections from README.md but only verifies outbound link validity via CI gate; it does not audit inbound references (anchor links in other docs, CLAUDE.md, or external materials) pointing to the sections being deleted, risking stranded references.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'No dead links (passes the docs link/index CI gate)' and Scope — 'Identify command/tool/config catalogs in README.md … and replace them with a short pointer + link.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-step: `grep -r 'README.md#' docs/ CLAUDE.md contributing/ ...` to enumerate all inbound anchor references to README sections; include updating those references in scope or confirm the CI gate covers bidirectional link checking.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in the plan whether inbound links to removed README anchor sections (e.g., `#commands`, `#configuration`) are an acceptable trade-off or require a redirect/tombstone comment, so the decision is explicit rather than silent.
findings.finding: The plan removes catalog sections from README.md (changing existing anchors) but does not acknowledge that external parties — other docs, blog posts, or users — may hold inbound links to the old catalog anchors (e.g., `README.md#commands`, `README.md#configuration`). The CI gate in AC3 validates outbound links FROM the README only, leaving silent inbound-link breakage unaddressed.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the Scope or AC: 'Inbound links to removed anchor sections are accepted as known breakage (external docs/bookmarks may need updating) — no redirects are added since README anchors cannot be server-redirected.' This makes the trade-off explicit without requiring new work.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/manifest-signing.md` to the Files list and specify that its `../README.md#configuration` anchor link must be updated (or verified to survive) when the Configuration catalog is trimmed.
findings.finding: docs/manifest-signing.md contains a hard inbound anchor link (`../README.md#configuration`) to the README's `## Configuration` section — one of the catalog sections the plan targets for trimming — but this consumer is not enumerated in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) keep the `## Configuration` section heading intact (just trim its key catalog body) so the anchor remains valid, or (b) update docs/manifest-signing.md to link to `docs/config.md` directly, and list that file in the plan's Files scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step to audit existing inbound anchor links (e.g. `grep -r 'README.md#' docs/` and `grep -r 'README.md#' CLAUDE.md`) before removing or renaming README sections, and ensure any removed anchors are either preserved as empty heading stubs or the referencing files are updated in the same PR.
findings.finding: The plan proposes removing or restructuring the `## Configuration` section (a volatile config catalog) but at least one inbound anchor link in the repo already references `../README.md#configuration` (in `docs/manifest-signing.md`). The plan's scope and AC are silent on inbound anchor breakage: if the section is removed or its heading renamed, that cross-doc link silently rots — and the non-existent link gate (AC3) would not catch it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the expected generated reference file paths/URLs (e.g. `docs/cli-reference.md`, `docs/mcp-reference.md`) in the plan so the executor can write correct links without consulting sibling tickets.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify the file paths or URLs of the generated CLI/MCP reference pages that will serve as link targets. An executor must guess or look up these paths from dependency tickets, which could produce incorrect links that pass a link-checker only if those paths are also missing.
findings.location: Scope bullet: 'replace them with a short pointer + link'; AC1: 'replaced with links'
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a 'Generated reference targets' subsection listing the expected output paths from the CLI/MCP generator tickets, e.g.:
- CLI reference: `docs/cli-reference.md`
- MCP reference: `docs/mcp-reference.md`
This also prevents a defect-taxonomy type (9): referencing a sibling ticket's files with no dependency edge.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out note (e.g., 'if generated references are found incomplete, revert this commit via git revert <sha> before merging') or describe a phased removal strategy so the destructive README edit has a named recovery path.
findings.finding: The plan performs a destructive content removal from README.md (command/tool/config catalogs) but does not state an explicit rollback or back-out path. The expand-contract sequencing (depends_on generators) is acknowledged, but the T4 criterion requires a named reversibility mechanism — not just sequencing acknowledgement.
findings.location: What / Scope / Acceptance Criteria (no rollback section present)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' or 'Risk' section noting: 'If generated references are incomplete at merge time, this PR should be reverted via `git revert`. The removed catalog content remains recoverable from git history at <predecessor commit>.' This satisfies T4's reversibility requirement without adding process overhead.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact file paths (e.g. `docs/cli-reference.md`, `docs/mcp-reference.md`) that the dependency tickets will produce, so the replacement link targets in this plan are unambiguous and verifiable at execution time.
findings.finding: The plan's core work — replacing catalog sections with 'links to the generated references' — assumes the generated CLI and MCP reference pages will exist and be at known, stable URLs/paths by the time this ticket executes. However, no generated reference files (e.g. docs/cli-reference.md, docs/mcp-reference.md) or reference-generator scripts (gen_cli_ref.py, etc.) were found anywhere in the repository. The plan relies on the depends_on predecessor tickets having landed these pages, but those pages' target paths are unspecified and unverifiable from the current codebase, meaning an executor cannot write the replacement links without knowing where the generated pages will live.
findings.location: Scope section — 'replace them with a short pointer + link'; What section — 'replace them with links to the generated references'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact file paths (or URL anchors) of the generated CLI and MCP reference pages that the README pointer links will target, so the executor can write those links without consulting the dependency tickets.
findings.finding: The plan names no file paths or URLs for the generated CLI/MCP reference pages that README.md will link to. An executor must discover those paths from the upstream dependency tickets, leaving the link targets unspecified in this plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note such as: 'The generated references land at `docs/cli-reference.md` and `docs/mcp-reference.md` (paths defined by the dependency tickets); README links should point there.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
